reasoning: The market asks if 10-12 SpaceX Starship launches will successfully reach space in 2025. Based on multiple reporting sources up to October/November 2025, SpaceX has only conducted four Starship test flights in 2025 (Flight 7 in January, Flight 8 in March, Flight 9 in May, and Flight 10 in August). As of October 6, 2025, there have only been 4 total Starship launches in the year. Achieving 10 launches by the end of December would require at least 6 more launches in the last three months, which is physically impossible given their current launch cadence (averaging roughly one flight every couple of months). Therefore, it is virtually certain this market will resolve to 'No'.
